title: VENEZIA. FONDACO DEI TEDESCHI
tags: ve venezia fondacodeitedeschi bar loungebar alajmo ristorante restaurant fontegodeitedeschi gruppodfs bancone barman clienti clientela ozio tavolini alchiuso locale dallalto panoramica caffè sala salone ambiente pavimento architettura canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d
description: OZIO & GEOMETRIE   Pare proprio che a Venezia gli Alajmo si siano trovati bene. Perché dopo aver messo la propria firma in Piazza San Marco, con il caffè Quadri la famiglia stellare ha aperto il nono ristorante del gruppo. Lo ha fatto all’interno del nuovo tempio del lusso lagunare, il T-Fontego dei Tedeschi, un megastore del gruppo DFS (divisione del colosso francese del lusso Lvmh) di 7000 metri quadrati distribuiti su quattro piani, vicinissimi al Canal Grande. L’arredamento è un omaggio a Venezia: bancone e divani (dagli schienali alti capaci di garantire intimità e rilassatezza) sono ispirati al felze delle gondole, i vetri sono ovviamente di Murano mentre il Carnevale è richiamato da fantasie alle pareti.   CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

title: VENEZIA. IL BAR AL FONDACO DEI TEDESCHI.
tags: bar emergenza coronavirus ve venezia venecia venice venedig locale fondacodeitedeschi coppia couple duo allclosed tuttochiuso commercio crisi virus nordest canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d 1001nightsthenew
description: TUTTO CHIUSO. ALL CLOSED.   Emergenza coronavirus | Chiusi tutti negozi e ristoranti | Aperti alimentari e farmacie „Nuove misure per l'emergenza coronavirus. Il presidente del consiglio, Giuseppe Conte, ha disposto, nella serata di oggi, mercoledì 11 marzo 2020, la chiusura di tutte le attività commerciali ad eccezione dei negozi di alimentari, delle farmacie e delle parafarmacie. Chiusi negozi, bar, pub, ristoranti (ferma restando la possibilità di consegna a domicilio), parrucchieri, centri estetici, mense. reparti aziendali non indispensabili per la produzione. Le industrie potranno continuare a lavorare a condizione che assumano necessarie misure di sicurezza che impediscano il contagio. Restano garantiti i trasporti e i servizi bancari, postali, finanziari e assicurativi, nonché le attività del settore agricolo e zootecnico. Aperti anche edicole, tabaccai, meccanici, benzinai nonché i liberi professionisti e le imprese che offrono servizi essenziali, come ad esempio gli idraulici. Per quanto riguarda i mercati rionali, invece, la decisione spetterà ai sindaci.“ ------------------------------------------------------  Coronavirus emergency | All shops and restaurants closed Open food and pharmacies „New measures for the coronavirus emergency. The chairman of the board, Giuseppe Conte, ordered today, Wednesday 11 March 2020, the closure of all commercial activities with the exception of grocery stores, pharmacies and parapharmacies. Closed shops, bars, pubs, restaurants (without prejudice to the possibility of home delivery), hairdressers, beauty centers, canteens. business departments not indispensable for production. Industries will be able to continue working on the condition that they take necessary safety measures that prevent contagion. Transport and banking, postal, financial and insurance services are guaranteed, as well as the activities of the agricultural and zootechnical sector. Newsagents, tobacconists, mechanics, petrol stations as well as freelancers and companies offering essential services, such as plumbers, are also open. As for the local markets, however, the decision will be up to the mayors. "  ------------------------------------------------------------------------  CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 70-300 f./4-5,6 IS USM

title: VENEZIA. FONDACO DEI TEDESCHI.
tags: ve venezia venecia venedig venice serenissima fondacodeitedeschi fondaco palazzo palace amo alajmo store negozio boutique shopping moda cortile atrio ristorante restaurant bar commercio rialto italia italy italie italien bellitalia canon eos6dmarkii 6dmarkii canoneos6dmarkii canon6dmarkii franco600d fotocomposizione archi volte architettura turisti tourist clienti
description: IL QUADRATO DEL LUSSO MONDIALE.   Situato ai piedi del Ponte di Rialto di Venezia, nell’atrio di uno spazio di rara bellezza, il rinnovato T Fondaco dei Tedeschi, AMO è il frutto della collaborazione tra i fratelli Alajmo e il designer Philippe Starck.  Il palazzo è uno dei centri per lo shopping di lusso più belli e cool del mondo, merito di una location unica, che racchiude nelle sue mura antiche, silenziose testimoni di secoli di scambi commerciali, 7.000 metri quadri di negozi e boutique che espongono accessori di moda, orologi, gioielli, prodotti beauty ma anche un’ampia selezione di artigianato locale, cibo e vini. Costruito nel XIII come punto d’approdo per le merci trasportate dai mercanti tedeschi, il palazzo venne interamente ricostruito nel 1508, dopo la sua distruzione causata da un incendio. Nel corso dei secoli, la destinazione d’uso del Fondaco dei Tedeschi è mutata e con essa è cambiato anche l’aspetto strutturale del palazzo: sono state demolite, ad esempio, le torrette laterali, mentre il cortile interno è stato coperto.   FOTOCOMPOSIZIONE realizzata con l'unione di 18 immagini.   CANON EOS 6D Mark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

title: VENEZIA. BAR DEL CENTRO.

description: I TEMPI MORTI POSSONO ESSERE VIVI.   Venezia ospita fra i 23 e i 25 milioni di visitatori all’anno. A questi vanno sommati i “gitanti giornalieri” (con treno e auto) e poi i circa 3 milioni l’anno di cosiddetti «falsi escursionisti» (chi pur visitando la città pernotta in terraferma). Venezia è costruita tutta in superficie, è appoggiata sul fango, i suoi abitanti sono le persone più superficiali del mondo: non vuol dire che siano frivoli, ma sono costretti a fare i conti con la realtà esteriore, senza nicchie dove rintanarsi a preservare la loro identità. Le altre città hanno metropolitane, cantine, catacombe, bunker. Qui non ci sono rifugi antituristi per proteggersi dai bombardamenti aerei di comitive low cost.  Non c’è scampo. Trenta milioni di turisti all’anno, in una post-città in cui tutto è sempre di più in funzione loro. Gli abitanti sono sempre di meno. Oggi siamo cinquantatremila. Come Gallarate, Velletri, Aversa. Venezia è già stata venduta. Intorno a San Marco il 99% dei ristoranti è gestito da cinesi, albanesi e mediorientali. Nel resto della città solo la metà è in mano a imprenditori locali   CANON EOS 6DMark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

Nel resto della città solo la metà è in mano a imprenditori locali   CANON EOS 6DMark II con ob. CANON EF 24-85 f./3,5-4,5 USM

title: Behind Bars - the view from the accountant's room
tags: venice italy window glass bar italia palace pane palazzo venezia ducale doges
description: The Doge's Palace (Italian: Palazzo Ducale) is a palace built in Venetian Gothic style, and one of the main landmarks of the city of Venice, northern Italy. The palace was the residence of the Doge of Venice, the supreme authority of the Republic of Venice, opening as a museum in 1923. Today it is one of the 11 museums run by the Fondazione Musei Civici di Venezia. en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doge%27s_Palace,_Venice AIMG_4801

description: Italy, Venice, “Basilica di Santa Maria della Salute”, commonly known simply as “La Salute”, is one of the largest churches of Venice & has the status of a minor basilica. It stands at the junction between the Canale Grande & the “Bacino di San Marco” on the lagoon. In October 1630, the Venetian Senate decreed that if the city was delivered from the currently raging plague that had killed about a third of Venice's population, then a new church would be built and dedicated to the Virgin Mary.  The city was so delivered & Baldassare Longhena, then only 26 years old, was selected to design the new church. It was consecrated in 1681, the year before Longhena's death & completed in 1687.

title: Cantine del Vino Già Schiavi
tags: venice italy shop bar italia wine venezia cantina bacaro
description: Very close to our hotel, this is probably the best wine store that I have  been to, but the true brilliance lies in being both a bar where you can enjoy a glass of wine and some snacks with being a bottle store. Apparently this is an old Venetian concept known as "bacaro". The shopkeeper and bartender almost managed to get me drunk the last Sunday morning of our visit when I came in for a bottle of grappa, by making me taste three half full glasses of recommended grappa varieties and finish them before letting me go. One of my favourite places in Venice.

title: Phot.Venice.Gondoliere.Leisure.01.041028.6164.jpg
tags: nikon d300 d200 square places place plaza plätze bar market venice venedig venezia
description: Italy, Venice , Gondolieri at afternoon leisure time,  parked at Fondamento Orseo, next to San Marcos square.  There are a few thousand gondoliers in Venice, a 30-minute ride usually costs € 100 by Gondola, regardless of the number of people, whether one or up to six person,  There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license. A license is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondolier depending on the district can cost up to 300.000 €…& more, on the other hand, a Gondolier makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010.  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly between 25.000€ & 35.000€ To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.   The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, built since about 1000 years, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, Venice’s only female Gondoliera, Alexandra Hai from Germany, known as Alex & as the rebel of the 21 century in Venice. She gave the local gondoliers a hard time never giving up her goal to become a gondoliera.   A court order of the administrative court allows her to work as a “Private Gondoliera”, at the same time not accepting the mafia manners which rule the city.  The building in the back is the Campo Franceso Morosini.  The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.   👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16 million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.   The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, built since about 1000 years, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law. Supported by the church, this law prescribed uniform black equipment for all gondolas; there were exceptions for foreign envoys & for festivals.  👉 One World one Dream, 🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over  16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Italy, Venice, the first & original "Harry’s Bar", opened by Giuseppe Cipriani in Venice in March 1931, named after ”Harry Pickering” who gave Cipriani, a Bartender at the Hotel Europe, the first money to open his own bar, today there are numerous franchises worldwide,   Guiseppe Cipriano is also the creator, around in the forties, of the classic long drink “Bellini”, marrow from sun-ripened pink peaches & Prosecco Spumante the drink is simple but great & at 22€, 2010, a cocktail highly overpriced like all other cocktails at Harris Bar too.  By the way, the “Beef Carpaccio”, created at the five star hotel Cipriani & owner of the “Harry’s Bar”, still served in the original way, not thinly sliced on a meat slicing machine, rather the raw beef tenderloin is flattened between foil, seasoned with a creamy kind of mayonnaise dressing & served with a small side salad at over 60 € is also absolutely highly overpriced, even so it was good, ... in general food & beverage is tasty & well prepared,… however, prices are in no relation to quality & value,… well,... "Hemmingway's Ghost" makes  it possible.  📌... If it would not have been for Hemmingway, who choose this place as his favoured bar in Venice as his hangout & consequently also by many other famous customers from culture, art, show business/Hollywood & business “joint the club”. My main reason to visit the Harry’s Bar was simply because of my curiosity for food & beverage & gastronomical historically well-known locations. Anyway, ... description: Italy, Venice , Gondolieri at afternoon leisure time,  parked at Fondamento Orseo, next to San Marcos square.  There are a few thousand gondoliers in Venice, a 30-minute ride usually costs € 100 by Gondola, regardless of the number of people, whether one or up to six person,  There are officially 425 Gondolas, 2010, in Venice with a license. A license is hard to get & stays normally in the family. A license for a Gondolier depending on the district can cost up to 300.000 €…& more, on the other hand, a Gondolier makes between 10 & 15 thousand € a month, …stand 2010.  Modern gondolas are up to 11 mtr long, 1.6 mtr wide & a weight of about 350 kg, made of 280 hand-made pieces using eight types of wood. It takes about 200 hours to manufacture a gondola; at a cost of roughly between 25.000€ & 35.000€ To compensate for unilateral propulsion, the gondola body is built asymmetrically along the central axis; the left side is curved more than the right starboard side, so that its contour on the starboard side is about 0.25 m shorter than on the port side. The several mtr long oar-paddle is held in an o complicated shaped fork, known as a Fórcola, which is inserted into a rectangular opening in the hull on the starboard side.  The Forcola is allowing several positions of the oar for slow forward rowing, powerful forward rowing, turning, slowing down, rowing backwards stopping, shaped individually for each gondoliers style.   The ornament iron, called the "fero da prorà" or "dolfin", on the front of the boat is made from brass, stainless steel, or aluminium. It serves as decoration; every detail of the gondola has its own symbolism & needed to balance the weight of the gondolier at the stern, has an "S" shape symbolic of the twists in the Canal Grande. Under the main blade, there is a kind of comb with six teeth or prongs pointing forward standing for the six districts of Venice. A kind of tooth juts out backwards toward the centre of the gondola symbolises the island of Giudecca, the curved top signifies the Doge's cap.  Originally, built since about 1000 years, the designs were not uniform & changed over the centuries. Originally, there were gondolas in all possible colours. The Venetian noble & patrician houses at the time tried to outdo each other in the magnificent equipment of the boats. In order to put an end to this unbridled pomposity, the Senate of Venice in 1562 passed the expense law.
